text: Abstract Background There are only a few openly accessible databases that store neuroimaging statistical maps. When studies are published the data are often greatly diminished: a few hundred coordinates are printed in supplemental tables and the three-dimensional aspects of the findings are relegated to two-dimensional figures. Much of this information is lost to future studies that would like to consider similar effects in prior literature. Results We present a web application, NeuroVault (neurovault.org), that allows researchers to store, search, view and overlap statistical maps. A wealth of neuroimaging data can be presented in a fully unthresholded three-dimensional form and analyzed using online tools built directly into the web application. NeuroVault connects to the Neurosynth database (neurosynth.org) to enable cognitive decoding of new data (i.e. relating a voxel-wise statistical map back into a cognitive domain) and uses a public REST API so that data in the database may be downloaded, processed and re-uploaded by other neuroinformatics tools. Conclusions NeuroVault allows for the enhanced archiving and reuse of neuroimaging results that are increasingly being reported at the level of whole volumes (i.e. image-based results). We hope that the database will promote increased transparency, data archival and ultimately the cumulative progress of the field.
